headline: Historian Pascal Blanchard: 'Identity will be one of the main themes of France's presidential campaign'
description: The issue of identity, tackled head-on by both the far-right and the radical left, calls for political solutions to heal divisions as the 2027 presidential election approaches, explains Pascal Blanchard, an expert in colonization, in an interview.

headline: Zelensky urges EU membership for Ukraine at Cyprus summit
description: After approving a €90 billion loan, European leaders gathered in Ayia Napa on Thursday for an informal summit. They remain cautious about opening accession negotiations and refuse to grant Ukraine any shortcuts or fast-track procedures.

headline: Bernard Arnault defuses speculation over his succession at LVMH shareholders meeting
description: At 77 years old, the CEO of the luxury group refuses to disclose who will replace him.

headline: Are language disorders on the rise in France? Concern grows in the era of screens
description: Testing among young children has expanded significantly in recent years, but there is a lack of studies to assess their prevalence.

headline: Sarkozy and the mystery of the Libyan money: A look back at a legal marathon
description: The appeals trial over suspicions of Libyan funding for the 2007 presidential campaign began on March 16. The case is filled with a cast of shady figures.

headline: 'I was so exhausted, I started hitting myself. They laughed': Four Cuban asylum seekers recount their ordeal at Europe's borders
description: The European Court of Human Rights is expected to rule in the coming weeks on the pushbacks experienced by four Cubans at the border between Lithuania and Belarus, against the backdrop of a hybrid war with Russia.
